description: Analyze the coordination of phosphothreonine 160 by the arginine triad in the active CDK2-Cyclin A complex. Fetch PDB: 1FIN into PyMOL. Isolate CDK2 (Chain A) and locate the phosphorylated Threonine 160 (residue name TPO). Identify the three conserved Arginine residues in Chain A (Arg 50, Arg 126, Arg 150) that coordinate the phosphate group. Measure at least three distances between the TPO phosphate oxygens and the guanidinium nitrogens of the three Arginines. Render a high-resolution, publication-quality PNG figure showing the phosphothreonine and the coordinating arginines as sticks, with distance dashes visible. Save the figure to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/cdk2_activation.png. Write a plain-text report documenting the interacting residues and the measured distances in Angstroms to ~/PyMOL_Data/cdk2_phospho_report.txt.
